dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3846 IS: Employer-Directed Skills Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Budd (for himself, Mr. Cassidy, and Mr. Husted) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions

form.official-title: To amend the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act regarding employer-directed skills development, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Employer-Directed Skills Act.

paragraph.text: Section 3 of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (29 U.S.C. 3102) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by redesignating paragraphs (15) through (18) as paragraphs (14) through (17), respectively; and
by inserting after paragraph (17), the following:

paragraph.text: The term employer-directed skills development means a program—

subparagraph.text: that is selected or designed to meet the specific skill demands of an employer (including a group of employers);

that is conducted pursuant to the terms and conditions provided under an employer-directed skills contract described in section 134(c)(3)(I), including the commitment by the employer to employ an individual upon successful completion of the program; and
for which the employer pays a portion of the cost of the program that is—

clause.text: determined by the local board involved; and

subclause.text: 10 percent of the cost, in the case of an employer with 50 or fewer employees;
25 percent of the cost, in the case of an employer with more than 50 but fewer than 100 employees; and
50 percent of the cost, in the case of an employer with 100 or more employees.

paragraph.text: Section 108(b)(4)(B) of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (29 U.S.C. 3123(b)(4)(B)) is amended by striking customized training programs and inserting employer-directed skills development.

subsection.text: Section 116(b)(2)(A) of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (29 U.S.C. 3141(b)(2)(A)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in clause (i), by striking subclause (VI) and inserting the following:

item.text: the number of program participants who, before exiting the program during a program year, completed on-the-job training or employer-directed skills development; to
the total number of program participants who received training services and who exited the program during that program year.

subsection.header: On the job training, employer-Directed skills development, incumbent worker training, and other training exceptions
subsection.text: Section 122(h) of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (29 U.S.C. 3152(h)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in the heading, by striking customized training and inserting employer-Directed skills development ;
in paragraph (1), by striking customized training and inserting employer-directed skills development; and

in paragraph (2), by striking the first sentence and inserting A one-stop operator in a local area shall collect the minimum amount of information from providers of on-the-job training, employer-directed skills development, incumbent worker training, internships, paid or unpaid work experience opportunities, and transitional employment as is necessary to enable the use of State administrative data to generate such performance information as the Governor may require..

subsection.text: Section 134(a)(2)(B)(v)(II) of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (29 U.S.C. 3174(a)(2)(B)(v)(II)) is amended by striking customized training and inserting employer-directed skills development.

subsection.header: Allowable statewide employment and training activities
subsection.text: Section 134(a)(3)(A)(i) of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (29 U.S.C. 3174(a)(3)(A)(i)) is amended by striking customized training and inserting employer-directed skills development.
Section 134(c)(3) of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (29 U.S.C. 3174(c)(3)) is amended—

subparagraph.text: in clause (i), in the matter preceding subclause (I), by striking clause (ii) and inserting clauses (ii) and (iii);
by redesignating clause (iii) as clause (iv); and
by inserting after clause (ii) the following:

subclause.text: A one-stop operator or one-stop partner shall not be required to conduct an interview, evaluation, or assessment of an individual under clause (i)(I) if such individual—

item.text: is referred by an employer to receive on-the-job training or employer-directed skills development in connection with that employer; and

has been certified by the employer as being in need of training services to obtain unsubsidized employment with such employer and having the skills and qualifications to successfully participate in the selected program of training services.

subclause.text: A one-stop operator or one-stop partner shall follow the priority described in subparagraph (E) to determine whether an individual that meets the requirements of subclause (I) of this clause is eligible to receive training services.

paragraph.text: in subparagraph (D), by striking clause (xi) and inserting the following:
in subparagraph (G)(ii)(II), by striking customized training and inserting employer-directed skills development; and
by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: An employer may receive a contract from a local board to provide employer-directed skills development to a participant if the employer submits to the local board an application that provides—

clause.text: the provider of the skills development program, which may be the employer;
the length of the skills development program;
the recognized postsecondary credential that will be awarded to, or any occupational skill that will be gained by, the program participant;
the cost of the skills development program;
the amount of such cost that will be paid by the employer (as described in section 3(19)(C)); and
a commitment by the employer to employ the participating individual upon successful completion of the program.
